title: APSPDCL to provide prepaid smart meters for government services from June 1
content: A whopping 1.17 lakh services are likely to be brought under this prepaid metering system, which includes 91,306 single-phase and 26,507 three-phase services

title: PM Modi arrives in Netherlands on second leg of five-nation tour
content: Mr. Modi said he would hold talks with Prime Minister Jetten and call on King Willem-Alexander and Queen Maxima

title: Jammu and Kashmir CM Omar Abdullah approves ₹93 crore power infrastructure projects
content: The approval of these projects was accorded during the sixth meeting of the Council of Ministers, the J&K CM said

title: Insensitivity and lack of concern, alleges Congress on Centre’s silence over U.P. storm deaths
content: Congress leader Ajay Rai said that the Union Government failed to respond appropriately to the crisis, especially as Russian President expressed condolences to India after 111 deaths and widespread destruction in U.P.

title: CS wants officials to speed up process for high-speed rail corridors; Railways urge govt to acquire land for rail depots
content: Telangana’s Chief Secretary urges swift land acquisition for high-speed rail corridors to enhance connectivity and economic development

title: UDF to finalise Cabinet formation on Saturday
content: Key decisions regarding the new government, including Cabinet formation and portfolio allocation, will be finalised at the meeting, says convener Adoor Prakash

title: Farmers from Punjab protest at Mohali-Chandigarh boundary against Centre Government
content: Farmers alleged the Central Government is encroaching on Punjab’s water through amendments to the Bhakra Beas Management Board rules, the Dam Safety Act, the Water Amendment Act 2024 and the Punjab Reorganisation Act

title: One month of Samrat Choudhary Government in Bihar
content: Decisions like Satellite Townships, the Police Didi Scheme, a Three-Tier Public Support System, one model school per block, regulating private schools, prioritising Bihari contractors, and implementing a paperless e-registration system for land aim to reduce corruption, save time, and enhance transparency

title: Rajasthan seeks permanent membership in Bhakra Beas Management Board
content: The Minister said appropriate decisions regarding water storage up to the maximum reservoir level of dams under the BBMB should be taken expeditiously after the completion of necessary studies

title: Elgar case: NIA seeks cancellation of bail of activists Sudha Bharadwaj and Varavara Rao
content: The NIA said that Bharadwaj and Rao attended a meeting on the Mumbai Press Club terrace on January 19, where other co-accused in the Elgaar Parishad case were also present

title: Four-year-old boy rescued after falling into borewell in Punjab’s Hoshiarpur
content: According to officials, the borewell had recently been made operational, and the work to fill gravel and soil around it was still pending

title: Supreme Court closes plea of Goa Congress leader after his death from snakebite
content: Appearing for the deceased leader, advocate Abhishek Jebraj requested the Court to leave the larger question of law open while closing the proceedings

title: DMRC rolls out hydrogen buses in Central Vista
content: The initiative seeks to promote green hydrogen as a clean fuel alternative and strengthen environmentally friendly public transport in the national capital

title: Joyride horse dies of highly contagious zoonotic disease disease in Chennai; Tamil Nadu goes into alert mode
content: ICAR centre advises State to immediately isolate and monitor all equines housed with the infected horse, recommends testing in adjoining areas; govt. to regulate inter-State movement of horses

title: Monsoon to reach Kerala on May 26, says IMD
content: Last year, the monsoon set in over Kerala on May 24 — its earliest arrival since 2009; IMD’s operational forecasts of the date of monsoon onset over Kerala during the past 21 years (2005-2025) were proved to be correct except in 2015

title: Bay system to trigger wet spell, ease heat in Tamil Nadu for next few days
content: Many parts of the State may receive heavy to moderate rainfall on Saturday; weather system may influence wet conditions till May 19, says RMC chief

title: Growing ‘attacks’on the judiciary by ‘parasites’, CJI Surya Kant says
content: “There are parasites in society who attack the system... they don’t get any employment and don’t have any place in the profession. Some of them become media, some of them become social media, some of them become RTI activists, and they start attacking everyone,” the CJI observed

title: Nominations open for innovation awards
content: Interested institutions and individuals can apply for the awards by submitting their entries on or before 22nd June 2026 at www.ckinnovationawards.in or Call +91 9444700068 to register

title: Film industry should avoid wasteful expenditure, but not at the cost of workers, says Kamal Haasan
content: Calling for an industry-wide conversation, he says together, they must evolve practical and sustainable operating practices for efficient film-making

title: Around 100 school vehicles tested unfit in Vellore, nearby districts
content: On Friday, Collector K. Tharpagaraj inspected fitness verification of school vehicles in Police Quarters’ playground in Tiruvannamalai town in the presence of Muniraj, Chief Educational Officer (CEO), Tiruvannamalai

title: Results show it is possible to raise quality without failing students: Sivankutty
content: Sivankutty congratulates students who have become eligible for higher studies after completing Class X

title: Bus crew, commuters escape unhurt after government bus catches fire in Ranipet
content: The police removed the charred vehicle from the carriageway to resume traffic movement on the stretch

title: Seminar on influence of Western Ghats on farming and economy held
content: Principal Secretary of Department of Forest, Environment and Ecology Srinivasulu said that despite high ecological and scientific significance of the Western Ghats, commoners living around Western Ghats were yet to fully appreciate its value for the future of humanity.

title: Election Commission orders probe into voting by foreign nationals in Tamil Nadu Assembly elections
content: A report has been sought from the returning officers of at least two cities and five districts across the State on how the foreigners managed to vote despite the Special Intensive Revision of the electoral rolls

title: 441 engineering students get placements at Kingston College near Katpadi
content: The event witnessed students from various departments take part in interviews conducted by private companies

title: Finance Ministry increases export levy on petrol, reduces that of diesel and ATF
content: There has been no change in the existing excise duty rates for petrol and diesel for domestic consumption

title: Differences over Kerala Chief Minister selection will be resolved smoothly, says K.C. Venugopal
content: When asked whether Ramesh Chennithala will be part of the Cabinet,  Venugopal says the matter will be discussed by the party leadership

title: Muslim side to move SC against Bhojshala verdict as Hindu groups celebrate amid heavy security in Dhar
content: Dhar city Qazi Waqar Sadiq rejects the court’s recommendation that Muslim side can approach State government for alternate piece of land for mosque.

title: New I-T Act is taxpayer-centric, says Principal Chief Commissioner Vidisha Kalra
content: She encourages taxpayers, professionals, and trade bodies to actively utilise the I-T department’s digital initiatives and facilitation measures for seamless compliance and enhanced taxpayer service

title: State to tighten groundwater governance to prevent over exploitation of resource
content: Minister N. Uttam Kumar Reddy pushes for groundwater enforcement, recharge and regulatory reforms as the resource is over-exploited in 16 districts, including Hyderabad

title: Thiruvananthapuram registers 98% pass in SSLC exams
content: 32,218 students eligible for higher studies and 2,313 secure A+ grade in all subjects. City schools fare well.

title: Petrol and diesel prices hike updates: ‘Take votes first, then raise prices’, Congress attacks Modi
content: Amid pressure on fuel supplies, oil-marketing companies hiked petrol and diesel prices by ₹3 across all variants

title: Tamil Nadu govt. may gradually increase women’s rights grant
content: The amount may initially be increased from ₹1,000 to ₹1,500,  given the State’s financial situation, says a government representative

title: CBI conducts searches in Haryana bank scam case
content: “Searches were conducted at seven locations, including residential premises, business establishments and showrooms of jewellers, premises of suspected beneficiaries of the misappropriated government funds, and other private entities connected with the investigation,” CBI said

title: Pregnant woman burnt to death after moving car catches fire in Kozhikode
content: The incident took place around 9 p.m. when the woman was returning home along with her husband after a medical consultation

title: No question of putting such restrictions: PM Modi dismisses reports of govt mulling tax on foreign travel
content: The Prime Minister’s response follows a news report that suggested that the government was considering levying a tax or a cess or a surcharge on foreign travel but no final decision had been taken yet.

title: Bhojshala-Kamal Maula complex declared as temple by Madhya Pradesh High Court
content: The court said that the Muslim community may approach the State government for allotment of separate land in the district for construction of a mosque

title: Rijiju raises concern over alleged undeclared foreign trips of Rahul Gandhi
content: Kiren Rijiju asks the Congress leader to share details of travels with authorities concerned to avoid action for any violation of law

title: ‘Surgeries, finances, familial pressure’: NEET retest has aspirants fighting on many fronts
content: The retest date clashes with the UPSSSC Pharmacist (Bheshajik) Mains exam, scheduled under Preliminary Eligibility Test 2025, some aspirants pointed out.

title: From semiconductors to water - what’s on PM Modi’s Netherlands agenda
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi arrived in the Netherlands for the second leg of his five-nation tour, aiming to deepen bilateral ties. He will hold talks with Dutch Prime Minister Rob Jetten, call on King Willem-Alexander and Queen Maxima, and address the Indian community.

title: 'As a smart bowler, you keep repeating what works': GT's Prasidh Krishna
content: After a stuttering start to IPL 2026, Gujarat Titans have emerged as one of the tournament’s most complete sides, with a relentless bowling attack led by Kagiso Rabada, Mohammed Siraj, Prasidh Krishna. Speaking to the Teamofindia.com, Prasidh opens up on GT’s turnaround, the team’s hard-length bowling philosophy, Shubman Gill’s calm leadership and his own evolution after last season’s Purple Cap-winning campaign.

title: 'Calling to molest again': Lucknow professor offers to leak exam paper to girl student
content: Lucknow University's zoology assistant professor is in police custody following allegations of leaking exam papers and making inappropriate advances towards a female student. Audio recordings, submitted by the student, reportedly capture the professor discussing paper leaks and offering academic favours in exchange for meetings. The university initiated an internal probe before police registered an FIR.

title: Petrol, diesel price hike: App based gig workers call for nationwide strike
content: Gig workers are staging a temporary nationwide strike, halting app-based services from 12 pm to 5 pm on Saturday. This protest, organized by GIPSWU, targets rising fuel prices and inadequate payment rates, which are significantly impacting drivers' daily earnings. The union aims to highlight the financial strain caused by increased fuel costs and low payouts.

title: In-form Shreyas Iyer's numbers stand tall, but will they carry Punjab Kings into playoffs?
content: Shreyas Iyer's stellar IPL-2026 campaign, marked by consistent middle-order batting and composed chases, has positioned him for a potential return to the Indian T20I squad. Despite Punjab Kings' recent struggles, Iyer's individual performance, including five half-centuries, highlights his evolution as a reliable finisher and leader.

title: NEET UG 2026 re-exam: NTA opens correction window to modify address, select cities
content: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has announced a re-examination for NEET UG 2026 on June 21, 2026, following directions from the National Medical Commission. Candidates can update their present address and exam city preferences from May 15 to May 21, 2026. The exam will be conducted offline in 13 languages.

title: Meta's highest-paid employee is not happy with 'just money motivated' tag for his team
content: Meta SuperIntelligence Lab chief Alexandr Wang is pushing back on the idea that his team joined for the money. On the Core Memory podcast, Wang said the real draw was compute access, talent density, and research freedom—not the $100 million packages Meta reportedly offered. He also addressed the viral soup story involving Mark Zuckerberg and an OpenAI recruit.

title: Unable to pay Rs 2,000 rent, Gujarat man lets landlord, aide repeatedly rape wife and daughter
content: A disturbing incident in Morbi, Gujarat, has surfaced where a tenant allegedly permitted his landlord and a relative to repeatedly assault his wife and 13-year-old daughter due to unpaid rent. The wife's mother filed a complaint, leading to the landlord's arrest and judicial custody. Police are searching for absconding relatives involved in the heinous crime.

title: Explained: CSK's qualification chances in IPL 2026 playoff race
content: With playoffs in sight, Gujarat Titans and Royal Challengers Bangalore are strong contenders, boasting high qualification probabilities. Sunrisers Hyderabad also hold a solid chance. Meanwhile, Punjab Kings and Rajasthan Royals have a decent shot, while Chennai Super Kings' prospects have dimmed. Kolkata Knight Riders and Delhi Capitals face slim hopes.

title: Ex-Google CEO Eric Schmidt has message for engineers who are still coding the old way
content: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt declared the era of manual code writing is over, citing AI's explosive power. He believes programmers still using traditional methods are behind, as AI enables individuals to build powerful applications previously requiring large teams. This shift is causing a sell-off in software stocks, with companies needing to adapt quickly to survive.

title: '2 great countries': Trump hails meeting with Xi as 'G-2 moment'
content: President Trump declared his China visit a historic "G-2" success, highlighting major trade deals including Boeing aircraft sales and agricultural commitments. He also suggested China would refrain from aggressive action on Taiwan during his presidency, while Xi Jinping privately warned of confrontation over the island. Trump indicated China's willingness to help broker an end to the Iran conflict.

title: 4-year-old pulled out safely from borewell after 9-hour rescue operation in Punjab
content: A four-year-old boy was safely rescued from a newly dug borewell in Hoshiarpur, Punjab, after a challenging nine-hour operation. Multiple rescue agencies and local volunteers collaborated to pull the child out from a depth of 20 to 30 feet. The boy, who appeared stable after the rescue, was immediately taken to a hospital for examination.

title: Bermuda Triangle mystery decoded: Scientists discover massive clue underwater
content: A remarkable geological discovery beneath Bermuda, a massive, unique rock layer, may finally explain the island's unusual elevation. Scientists used earthquake waves to reveal this 'life raft' structure, which has kept Bermuda afloat for millions of years, unlike other volcanic islands. While not solving Triangle myths, this real, measurable anomaly offers a fascinating new perspective.

title: BTS breaks silence on historic FIFA stage with message of hope and unity
content: BTS will make history as co-headliners of the first-ever halftime show at the 2026 FIFA World Cup final on July 19 in New York New Jersey Stadium. Sharing the stage with Madonna and Shakira, the group said the performance is a chance to spread “hope and harmony” worldwide. BTS also highlighted their desire to support children’s education through the FIFA Global Citizen campaign.

title: Jannik Sinner and Laila Hasanovic’s 2026 net worth and earnings revealed
content: Jannik Sinner and Laila Hasanovic’s combined net worth in 2026 is estimated at around $35 million to $40 million. Most of that wealth comes from Sinner’s tennis prize money, ATP success, and endorsement deals with global brands. Hasanovic contributes through modeling, fashion partnerships, and social media promotions. Sinner’s growing success in international tennis continues driving the couple’s financial growth.

title: 'Talentless loser': Taylor Swift fans slam Joe Alwyn amid Kelce wedding buzz
content: Taylor Swift fans sparked fresh online drama after old Joe Alwyn headlines resurfaced on X. One viral fan comment claimed Alwyn “wouldn't have a dollar” without Swift’s support, triggering heated debate across social media. While some users defended the actor, many Swifties mocked his career struggles. The controversy comes as attention around Swift’s future with Travis Kelce and growing wedding rumours continues dominating entertainment headlines throughout 2026.

title: 'The only thing can harm us ... ': Rishabh Pant opens up
content: Despite a victory over Chennai Super Kings, Lucknow Super Giants captain Rishabh Pant expressed dejection over the team's disappointing IPL 2026 season. LSG, already eliminated, secured a seven-wicket win powered by Mitchell Marsh and Nicholas Pooran, but Pant highlighted frustrations with implementing the team's thought process effectively.

title: 'Are you heroine or...': Kannada actor Chaithra Achar gets targeted online; troll apologises later
content: Sandalwood actor Chaithra Achar bravely responded to a vulgar online troll by tagging Bengaluru City Police, leading to the troll deleting his post and apologizing. This incident highlights the ongoing cyberbullying faced by women in the entertainment industry, with past cases involving actors Ramya, Rajini, Karunya Ram, and Mansi Joshi.

title: ‘Multiple antemortem injuries’: Chilling details emerge in Noida dowry death case
content: A retired district judge and her advocate son were booked in Bhopal for the alleged dowry death of the latter's wife, Twisha Sharma. Sharma was found hanging at her marital home, with her family alleging dowry harassment and murder. The retired judge has since been granted anticipatory bail.

title: FIR against Abhishek Banerjee over inflammatory statements, remarks against Amit Shah
content: TMC MP Abhishek Banerjee faces an FIR for alleged inflammatory statements, including remarks on DJs and Union Home Minister Amit Shah. He accused the Centre of allowing post-poll violence in West Bengal and manipulating the election counting process, with party candidates and agents allegedly removed from centers.

title: After Trump’s Beijing visit, Taiwan detects Chinese naval vessels near its waters
content: Taiwan's defense ministry reported eight Chinese naval vessels near the island following US President Trump's China visit. Trump suggested China might act against Taiwan after his term, acknowledging Beijing's strong objections to its independence. The US arms package for Taiwan remains under review amid escalating tensions and China's territorial claims.

title: 'Ek din ka mukhya mantri': Jasprit Bumrah reacts after MI captaincy debut
content: Jasprit Bumrah captained Mumbai Indians for the first time in IPL 2026, leading them to a win against Punjab Kings. Drawing a parallel to the film 'Nayak', Bumrah expressed his enjoyment of the role. This experience adds to his prior captaincy stints with the Indian national team in Tests and T20Is.

title: Major guns down JCO during firing practice at Maharashtra's Pulgaon base
content: A commissioned army officer, Major Manan Tiwari, allegedly shot and killed his junior, Subedar Major Om Bahadur Khand, at close range with an assault rifle during a firing practice session at the Pulgaon Central Ammunition Depot. The incident, described as accidental by an army spokesperson, resulted in multiple bullet wounds for the deceased JCO.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' OTT release: Netizens reveal biggest changes to Ranveer Singh starrer
content: Ranveer Singh's action drama 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has premiered online internationally on Netflix, revealing a 'raw and undekha' version. Netizens are actively comparing this streaming cut with the Indian theatrical release, highlighting more graphic and extended violent scenes, including a famed 'hammer scene' and a gory football sequence.

title: 'Karuppu' box office collections day 1: Suriya and Trisha Krishnan starrer opens strong
content: Suriya's 'Karuppu' has made a powerful debut at the box office, raking in ₹20.66 crore worldwide on its opening day. The Tamil version led the charge with strong occupancy, particularly in evening and night shows. While the film is a commercial success, critical reviews offer a mixed perspective on its narrative.

title: Abu Dhabi to ramp up crude storage, India’s reserve may go up 70%
content: India's crude oil security is set for a significant boost as the UAE's ADNOC agrees to increase crude storage to 30 million barrels. This move will add over four million tonnes to India's existing strategic reserves, enhancing energy supply stability amidst regional conflicts. Agreements also explore LNG and LPG storage, strengthening India-UAE energy ties.

title: Afridi clashes with Pakistan captain in dressing room after shock Bangladesh defeat
content: Pakistan's Test defeat to Bangladesh has ignited dressing-room friction, with captain Shan Masood reportedly clashing with pace spearhead Shaheen Shah Afridi over bowling speed and batting collapses. The loss, compounded by an ICC slow over-rate penalty, is expected to lead to significant team changes for the next Test.

title: Ex-merchant navy captain ‘sold’ youths from UP to cyber slavery hubs in Cambodia, Laos; held
content: A former merchant navy captain, Nagesh Kumar, has been apprehended for allegedly orchestrating a cyber slavery ring. He is accused of luring Indian youths with false job promises to Cambodia, where they were forced into fraudulent call centers. The racket, exposed by victims, involved trafficking individuals to Southeast Asia and exploiting them for financial gain.

title: Almost 13-hour flight delay leaves SpiceJet’s Delhi-bound flyers stranded in Pune
content: Over 100 SpiceJet passengers faced a grueling 13-hour delay on a Pune-Delhi flight. Initially scheduled for a 5:55 am departure, the flight was repeatedly rescheduled due to technical issues. Passengers endured long waits, with some opting for refunds and booking expensive last-minute tickets, while others spent over 16 hours at the airport.

title: No bail relief for Darshan, SC asks Karnataka to examine 60 key witnesses in 1 year
content: The Supreme Court has given Kannada actor Darshan a year to approach for bail if his trial in the Renukaswamy murder case shows no substantial progress. The court directed the Karnataka government to ensure 60 witnesses are examined within this period, urging the trial court to avoid adjournments on flimsy grounds.

title: Born in the USA? MAGA hawks go after desi doc's 'delivery' service
content: A US congressional inquiry has launched a spotlight on maternity businesses, including a clinic operated by an Indian-origin physician, amid accusations of promoting 'birth tourism.' Legislators assert that the practice takes advantage of immigration laws, enabling international families to secure citizenship for their infants in the United States.

title: Trump, Back From China, Preps Big Iran Strikes With Epic Fury 2.0: Report
content: The US and China have agreed that "Iran can't have nuclear weapons" and that the Strait of Hormuz must be opened, Donald Trump said

title: Rajasthan NEET Aspirant Dies By Suicide, Father Says Was Expecting 650 Marks
content: According to the police, the student allegedly hanged himself from a ceiling fan using his sister's scarf while one sister was attending coaching classes and the other was in the bathroom.

title: Provocative Speeches, Fuelling Enmity: Case Against Trinamool's Abhishek Banerjee
content: A complaint alleged that Abhishek Banerjee had delivered provocative speeches during the election campaign and had issued threats against Union Home Minister Amit Shah.

title: Lucknow University Professor Detained Over Paper Leak Allegations
content: The university administration said it follows a "zero-tolerance" policy in such matters and any conduct affecting the dignity of the institution and the academic environment would not be tolerated.

title: Why Xi Gave Trump A Rare Tour Of China's Most Guarded Political Compound
content: Zhongnanhai, named after two lakes located within the compound, is not only a workplace but also the residence of China's senior leadership.

title: 10 Principles From Ayodhya Verdict That Paved Way For Bhojshala Temple
content: The high court noted that in the present case, it is not deciding the title of the property of the disputed area.

title: Exclusive: Indian Air Force Officers Recount "Once In A Lifetime" Op Sindoor Mission
content: The success of the high-precision Operation Sindoor can be attributed to the dedication and focus of many personnel from the Indian armed forces.

title: "Nothing From China Allowed": Team Trump Dumps Chinese Passes, Burner Phones
content: While Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping kept up a cordial front on camera, relations between American and Chinese press and security teams were far more strained behind the scenes.

title: No Interim Relief For Union Minister Bandi Sanjay's Son In Sexual Assault Case
content: Justice T Madhavi Devi, who resumed hearing in the interim anticipatory bail petition of Kumar's son Bandi Bageerath on Friday evening, said she was not inclined to grant any interim order at this stage.

title: Only Electric Autos In Delhi Soon? Pollution Control Panel's Big Decision
content: With effect from October 1, 2026, no fuel shall be dispensed at fuel stations across the National Capital Region (NCR) to vehicles that do not possess a valid Pollution Under Control Certificate (PUCC).

title: 4-Year-Old Boy Rescued After Falling Into Borewell In Punjab
content: The boy, identified as Gurkaran Singh, was trapped inside the borewell at a depth of around 20 to 30 feet, officials said.

title: Boeing Confirms China Is Buying 200 Planes
content: Aerospace giant Boeing on Friday confirmed that China had committed to purchasing 200 aircraft, as previously announced by US President Donald Trump during his visit to Beijing.

title: US Plans To Unveil Criminal Charges Against Cuba's Raul Castro Next Week
content: Castro is the brother of the late Fidel Castro, the revolutionary and longtime foil of the United States who led the island's communist government for decades.

title: PM Modi Arrives In Netherlands To Deepen Bilateral Ties
content: PM Modi is scheduled to hold bilateral talks with the Dutch leadership in The Hague, focusing on expanding cooperation in trade, investment, semiconductors, clean energy, water management, agriculture, defence, and emerging technologies.

title: Lebanon, Israel Ceasefire Extended By 45 Days: US
content: Lebanon and Israel on Friday extended a ceasefire for 45 days, despite a new flareup in violence, the US State Department said after mediating talks.

title: US-Iran War News Live Updates: Iran's Araghchi Says "No Military Solution To Anything Related To Iran"
content: US-Iran War News Live Updates: US President Donald Trump is set to meet Chinese President Xi Jinping today in a high-stakes summit between the two countries.

title: Gautam Adani To Lead Groundbreaking Ceremony For Vision Care Initiative In Bihar
content: Organised jointly by the Adani Foundation and the Akhand Jyoti Eye Hospital, the event is expected to mark a major expansion in affordable rural healthcare infrastructure.

title: Trump China Visit LIVE Updates: Live: Trump Leaves China After High-Stake Summit Focused On Iran, Taiwan
content: Donald Trump's 2-Day China Visit Live Update: During the trip, US arms sales to Taiwan, China's controls on rare-earth exports, and the countries' raucous trade relationships are among the main issues expected to be taken up by Trump and Xi.

title: Israel Carries Out Strikes In Gaza, Targets Hamas Chief
content: Israel said on Friday that it carried out a strike in Gaza targeting Ezzedine Al-Haddad, who it described as chief of the armed wing of Palestinian movement Hamas.

title: Court Declares Bhojshala Site A Temple. Hindu Side's Lawyer Shares What's Next
content: The High Court cancelled the 2003 Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) order allowing Muslims to offer Friday prayers on Bhojshala premises.

title: Monsoon likely to hit Kerala on May 26: IMD
content: Vishwa Mohan is Senior Editor at The Times of India. He writes on environment, climate change, agriculture, water resources and clean energy, tracking policy issues and climate diplomacy. He has been covering Parliament since 2003 to see how politics shaped up domestic policy and India’s position at global platform. Before switching over to explore sustainable development issues, Vishwa had covered internal security and investigative agencies for more than a decade. Read More

title: CJI on panel to pick CEC not statutory need: Govt
content: Govt, in an affidavit, said the issue is not what the appointment process should be, but whether SC can examine Parliament’s chosen mechanism at all

title: Bhojshala case: Muslims may be offered alternative plot of land in Dhar as in Babri case
content: the court also struck down a 2003 archaeological survey of India (ASI) order that had restricted Hindu worship at the 11th century site to Tuesdays while permitting Muslim prayers at the monument on Fridays . the ruling validates Muslims’ fears, says jamiat The Union govt and the ASI have been directed to take over the administration and management of the site as a Bhojshala and Kamal Maula mosque complex .

title: Marsh lights up Lucknow, dents CSK's playoff push in LSG's seven-wicket victory
content: Photo/IPL At TOI Sports Desk, reporters work around the clock to bring you comprehensive updates from the world of sports. title: Brace for price rise after fuel hike; inflation may increase 10-25 bps
content: the increase in retail prices of petrol and diesel is expected to drive headline inflation print by 10-25 basis points (bps) in the coming months . several companies, which were holding on to price hikes, are now expected to raise rates, citing higher transport and input costs .

title: Jobless youngsters like cockroaches, attacking everyone, says CJI on plea
content: a lawyer had filed a petition questioning him whether he intended to join the parasite club . a bench of chief justice Surya Kant and justice Joymalya Bagchi was irritated with the lawyer . the bench allowed the advocate to withdraw his plea .

title: UAE announces $5bn investment in India as PM Modi meets Gulf nation prez
content: india and the UAE sought to boost their energy, defence shipping and technology ties, signing 6 MoUs and announcing investments worth $ 5 billion from the arab nation into India . modi reiterated India’s strong condemnation of the attacks on the UAE, without naming Iran .
